# vercel_feature_flag_segment (Data Source)

Provides information about an existing Feature Flag Segment.

This data source reads the simplified exact-match segment shape exposed by this provider and looks up the segment by its stable `slug` within a project.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "vercel_feature_flag_segment" "example" {
  project_id = "prj_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
  slug       = "internal-users"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `project_id` (String) The ID of the Vercel project that owns the segment.
- `slug` (String) The stable segment slug used by the Vercel Flags API.

### Optional

- `team_id` (String) The ID of the Vercel team. Required when configuring a team resource if a default team has not been set in the provider.

### Read-Only

- `description` (String) A human-readable description of the segment.
- `exclude` (Attributes Set) Exact entity attribute values that are always excluded from this segment. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--exclude))
- `hint` (String) An optional dashboard hint for the segment.
- `id` (String) The ID of the segment.
- `include` (Attributes Set) Exact entity attribute values that are always part of this segment. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--include))
- `name` (String) The human-readable segment name shown in the Vercel dashboard.

<a id="nestedatt--exclude"></a>
### Nested Schema for `exclude`

Read-Only:

- `attribute` (String) The entity attribute to match.
- `entity` (String) The entity type to match.
- `values` (Set of String) The exact values to include or exclude for this entity attribute.


<a id="nestedatt--include"></a>
### Nested Schema for `include`

Read-Only:

- `attribute` (String) The entity attribute to match.
- `entity` (String) The entity type to match.
- `values` (Set of String) The exact values to include or exclude for this entity attribute.
